_meta.issue: #112-#118 [M15] Formative path S4 — Holak Scale (training module)
_meta.interaction: Practice classification (kind: classify) — formative exercise, NOT scored toward completion
_meta.source: Holak Scale maturity axes: context management, token thrift. Attribution: quality-blog.eu/pl/blog/skala-holaka.
_meta.dataPolicy: ALL examples SYNTHETIC and generic (no real data/PII). Formative module — no pass threshold, no certificate.

intro: You spot higher AI maturity not by the number of tools, but by HOW you feed the model context — and what it costs. This module trains context hygiene and token-cost awareness. It is a growth exercise, not a test.

blocks.text: The model sees only what fits in the context window. Too little context → a generic or wrong answer; too much irrelevant content → the model loses the signal and token cost grows. Mature practice means giving exactly what is needed — no more, no less.

items: Context = the data, instructions and examples that truly affect the task.
Token cost grows with every character pasted — including the useless ones.
Repeatable tasks gain the most from templates and context hygiene.

blocks.title: A Holak Scale axis
blocks.text: Deliberate management of context and cost is one of the axes that separate „I use AI” from „I use AI maturely”. More: quality-blog.eu/pl/blog/skala-holaka.
Lean practice selects context and reuses proven templates. Wasteful practice pastes „everything just in case”, mixes topics, and describes the context from scratch every time.

blocks.title: The „just in case” trap
blocks.text: Pasting your whole knowledge base into every prompt does not raise accuracy — more often it blurs the task and raises cost. Dose context deliberately.
Next you will classify practices as lean or wasteful. There is no pass threshold here — the goal is to recognize a pattern you can carry over into your own work.

interaction.title: Lean or wasteful context practice?
interaction.intro: Sort each practice. This is a growth exercise — feedback explains why a practice is (not) lean.

categories.label: Lean practice
categories.desc: Selects context, controls cost, reuses templates.

categories.label: Wasteful practice
categories.desc: Adds excess, mixes topics, describes everything from scratch.

items.text: I pass only the document fragments relevant to the task.
items.rationale: Selecting context raises accuracy and lowers cost.

items.text: I paste my whole knowledge base into every prompt „just in case”.
items.rationale: Excess blurs the signal and raises token cost.

items.text: I keep a repeatable task as a prompt template with slots for data.
items.rationale: A template means repeatability and context hygiene.

items.text: I describe the context from scratch every time, in different words.
items.rationale: No template = wasted time and inconsistent results.

items.text: I remove stale and contradictory information from the context.
items.rationale: Clean context lowers the risk of error and hallucination.

items.text: I keep dozens of unrelated topics in a single chat.
items.rationale: Mixing topics clutters the context and confuses the model.

points: Give exactly the context that affects the task — no more.
Turn repeatable tasks into prompt templates.
Every pasted character has a cost — dose context deliberately.
checklist: Can I tell which parts of the context are truly needed?
Do I have a template for my most frequent AI task?
Do I clear stale and contradictory content from the context?
summary.nextStep: Next step: take one repeatable task and turn it into a prompt template with controlled context.
